items.title: The latest on the Mannheim car-ramming incident | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Two people were killed and several others injured when a car drove into a crowd in the city of Mannheim in south western Germany. A 40-year-old German man has been arrested and is being treated for injuries. Police say there's no evidence of any political motive. But Chancellor Olaf Scholz has called it a senseless act of violence.

00:00 The latest on the Mannheim car ramming
02:25 Akanksha Saxena, DW Reporter

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: At least two people dead after car ramming incident in Mannheim, Germany | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Police sources say at least two people have been killed and several others injured in a car ramming incident in south-western Germany.

The incident took place in the city of Mannheim. Authorities say they have taken a 40 year-old German person into custody. Officials are asking the public to stay away from the downtown area. The incident comes during the region's carnival season, when crowds gather for parades. 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: European leaders agree to draw up peace plan for Ukraine | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>European leaders say they have agreed to draw up a peace plan for Ukraine, following a summit in London on Sunday. Britain and France are leading the efforts with the aim of working together with the US. Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was in London for the meeting. He said he believes he can salvage his relationship with Donald Trump – and that he felt Europe's clear support. 

00:00 Europe draws peace plan for Ukraine 
00:57 DW Correspondents Rosie Birchard, Nick Connolly
08:02 Olexander Scherba, former Ukrainian Ambassador to Austria

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel has blocked the entry of aid into Gaza as it demands Hamas agree to a US plan for a ceasefire extension. The UN, several humanitarian aid providers and the Arab world have sharply criticized Israel for blocking the aid deliveries.

items.title: How might a UK-led 'coalition of the willing' defend Ukraine without a US backstop? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he believes he can salvage his relationship with Donald Trump despite their explosive meeting in the Oval Office on Friday.  Zelenskyy was speaking after a summit of European leaders in London aimed at drumming up support for Ukraine. The gathering ended with proposals to achieve a peace plan including forming a coalition of nations willing to send peacekeepers to Ukraine in the event of a ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Ukraine summit in London ends with proposals to put European troops on the ground in a post-ceasefire Ukraine
4:16 Angela Stent, Foreign policy analyst at the Brookings Institution, on why Zelenskyy should remain president

items.title: Ukraine summit outcome: Europe must do 'heavy lifting to defend itself' says Starmer | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer has said that a number of countries were ready to join Europe's new 'coalition of the willing' to help Ukraine.

Starmer made the comments at the conclusion of today's security summit in London, aimed at ending the war in Ukraine. More than a dozen high-ranking delegates attended the talks, including Ukraine's President Volodymr Zelenskyy. Efforts to resolve the war with Russia and bolster European defenses have taken on added urgency, as the US signals it's less willing to support Ukraine. 

Starmer stressed that Europe has to step up and do the 'heavy lifting to defend itself'.

Chapters:
0:00 High-ranking delegates attend talks in London on ending war in Ukraine 
2:22 DW Correspondent Teri Schultz on Keir Starmer's proposed 'coalition of the willing'
6:28 Yevheniia Kravchuk, Ukrainian lawmaker, ALDE Party Vice President, on Starmer's remarks for Europe to do the "heavy lifting" for security
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
11:58 DW Correspondent Nick Connolly in Kyiv on the Ukrainian reaction to the London summit
15:14 Outgoing German Chancellor Scholz's comments on Ukraine summit
17:16 DW Chief Political Editor Michaela Küfner on Scholz's comments on whether NATO will hold as an alliance
